task: review microbial transmission/colonization and succession from pregnancy through infancy and implications for disease prevention/intervention
abstract: The microbiome is associated with many diseases and is used as a biomarker and intervention target. This review summarizes evidence and mechanisms of microbial transmission and colonization from pregnancy to infancy, trajectories of microbiome succession, and key factors shaping early-life microbiota with implications for health and disease.
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: Tabular and latent space synthetic data generation: a literature review
task: review methods for synthetic tabular data generation and latent-space approaches, applications and evaluation considerations
model: tabular generative models (GAN/VAE/diffusion variants; latent-space methods)
abstract: Synthetic data generation supports anonymization, regularization, oversampling, and semi/self-supervised learning. This survey reviews algorithms for synthetic data generation, with an emphasis on tabular data (often overlooked), including approaches that generate in latent space and methods tailored to specific formats and ML tasks, summarizing trends and evaluation issues.
evaluation_design: literature review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=partial (generative modeling background; not LLM explainability).

title: New Materialism and Posthumanism in Roman Archaeology: When Objects Speak for Others
task: discuss integration of new materialism and posthumanism in Roman archaeology and implications for studying material culture and inequality
abstract: This paper discusses how theories from the ontological turn, posthumanism, and new materialism have been adopted in archaeology and argues that Roman archaeology needs deeper critical integration and theoretical refinement to realize their potential. It clarifies what new materialism is (beyond a “history of objects”) and suggests how it can broaden perspectives on material culture and social issues such as inequality, slavery, and coloniality.
evaluation_design: theory/position paper
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=no.

title: FaiRR: Faithful and Robust Deductive Reasoning over Natural Language
venue: ACL (Annual Meeting of the Association for Computational Linguistics)
task: deductive reasoning over natural-language rulebases with faithful proof-graph generation
model: Transformer components (rule selection, fact selection, knowledge composition)
abstract: Transformers can perform deductive reasoning over natural-language rulebases and can generate reasoning steps (proof graphs), but joint black-box generation can be unfaithful. This paper proposes a modular framework with separate rule selection, fact selection, and knowledge composition to enforce causal faithfulness from proof steps to inferences, and introduces FaiRR, showing robustness to language perturbations and faster inference than prior approaches.
explanation_techniques: explicit proof graphs / modular reasoning traces
evaluation_design: benchmark experiments on deductive reasoning datasets; robustness tests
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=partial (faithfulness/trace generation relevant; not LLM explainability per se).

title: Insects as bioindicator: A hidden gem for environmental monitoring
task: review insects as bioindicators for environmental assessment and monitoring
abstract: This review surveys how insects can serve as sensitive bioindicators of environmental quality and change. It summarizes indicator taxa and response signals across habitats, methods for sampling and interpreting insect-based indicators, and practical advantages and limitations for monitoring and conservation applications.
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: A Critical Historic Overview of Artificial Intelligence: Issues, Challenges, Opportunities, and Threats
venue: BONVIEW AI and AI (publisher/venue as in PDF)
task: overview of AI history, challenges (correlation vs causation), opportunities, and risks; proposes cybernetic AI framing
abstract: This article provides a historical overview of artificial intelligence, highlighting major issues, challenges, opportunities, and perceived threats. It discusses limitations such as confusion between correlation and causation and argues for incorporating cybernetics more centrally, proposing a “cybernetic AI” framing.
evaluation_design: overview / perspective
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=no.

title: Aminoacyl-tRNA synthetases in human health and disease
task: mini-review of aminoacyl-tRNA synthetases (aaRSs), canonical/noncanonical roles, and disease links
abstract: Aminoacyl-tRNA synthetases (aaRSs) are ancient enzymes required for translation, but many also have noncanonical functions. This mini-review summarizes aaRS biology, emerging roles beyond protein synthesis, and how aaRS variants and dysregulation contribute to human disease, highlighting diagnostic and therapeutic implications.
evaluation_design: mini review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: Circular RNA vaccine in disease prevention and treatment
task: review circRNA vaccines: design, delivery, immunogenicity, and applications vs linear mRNA vaccines
abstract: Circular RNAs (circRNAs) have covalently closed structures and can be engineered for medical applications. This review discusses circRNA vaccine design (e.g., IRES/ORF constructs), delivery, and immune responses, and contrasts potential advantages over linear mRNA vaccines such as improved stability and translation, outlining challenges and future directions.
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: Structural basis for substrate selection by the SARS-CoV-2 replicase
task: characterize how SARS-CoV-2 RNA polymerase complex selects substrates and implications for antiviral nucleoside analogues
abstract: The SARS-CoV-2 replication–transcription complex (RTC) is a target for antiviral nucleoside analogues. This study reports structural insights into how the viral RNA-dependent RNA polymerase and associated factors recognize correct substrates during RNA synthesis, informing mechanisms of fidelity and how nucleotide analogues such as remdesivir interact with the RTC.
evaluation_design: structural biology study (e.g., cryo-EM) + biochemical assays
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=no.

title: MedBench: A Large-Scale Chinese Benchmark for Evaluating Medical Large Language Models
task: benchmark evaluation of Chinese medical LLMs across exams and real-world diagnosis-style tasks
abstract: The rise of medical large language models (LLMs) motivates unified evaluation standards. This paper introduces MedBench, a large-scale Chinese benchmark spanning standardized medical exams and more realistic clinical case diagnosis settings, and reports extensive experiments analyzing current Chinese medical LLM performance and gaps between existing benchmarks and practical medical requirements.
evaluation_design: benchmark construction + multi-model evaluation
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=yes (LLM evaluation relevant; not primarily explanation methods).

title: Community knowledge, attitude, practices and beliefs associated with persistence of malaria transmission in North-western and Southern regions of Tanzania
task: qualitative assessment of community and provider knowledge/attitudes/practices/beliefs (KAP) and barriers linked to persistent malaria transmission
abstract: Despite declines, malaria remains a public health concern in Tanzania. This qualitative study uses in-depth interviews and focus group discussions across high-transmission regions to assess community and health-provider knowledge, attitudes, practices, and beliefs about malaria, identifying misconceptions and structural barriers (e.g., staffing, financing, care-seeking, net/IRS beliefs) that may contribute to persistent transmission.
evaluation_design: qualitative study (IDI + FGD; thematic analysis)
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=no.

title: Some models are useful, but how do we know which ones? Towards a unified Bayesian model taxonomy
task: propose PAD taxonomy for Bayesian models and utility dimensions for evaluating models holistically
model: Bayesian models (taxonomy PAD: distribution P, approximator A, data D)
abstract: Probabilistic (Bayesian) modeling has surged across sciences and industry, but a principled model-building workflow remains incomplete. This survey asks (a) what a Bayesian model is and (b) what makes a good Bayesian model. It proposes the PAD taxonomy (based on the assumed joint distribution P, posterior approximator A, and training data D) and discusses ten utility dimensions for holistic evaluation (e.g., causal consistency, recoverability, predictive performance, fairness, interpretability, robustness), with example decision trees for utility trade-offs.
evaluation_design: survey/methodology
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=partial (model utility/interpretability framing; not LLM explainability).

title: The Impact of Hospitality Work Environment on Employees’ Turnover Intentions During COVID-19 Pandemic: The Mediating Role of Work-Family Conflict
task: analyze how hospitality work environment affects turnover intentions mediated by work-family conflict during COVID-19
abstract: Employees’ turnover intentions and work–family conflict became salient challenges for hospitality organizations during COVID-19. This study examines the impact of the hospitality work environment on employees’ turnover intentions and tests the mediating role of work–family conflict, using empirical data to characterize relationships and managerial implications.
evaluation_design: survey-based empirical study (mediation analysis)
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=no.

title: The role of Th17 cells in inflammatory bowel disease and the research progress
task: review Th17 cells roles in inflammatory bowel disease pathogenesis and fibrosis; summarize research progress
abstract: Th17 cells play an important role in abnormal immune responses in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and contribute to inflammation and fibrosis. This review summarizes current understanding of Th17-related mechanisms in IBD progression and highlights recent research advances and therapeutic considerations.
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: Towards More Faithful Natural Language Explanation Using Multi-Level Contrastive Learning in VQA
task: improve faithfulness of post-hoc natural-language explanations for VQA using multi-level contrastive learning
abstract: Natural language explanation (NLE) in visual question answering aims to explain model decisions and improve user trust, but existing post-hoc methods can be unfaithful. This paper proposes a multi-level contrastive learning approach to encourage explanations that better align with the model’s decision process, reporting improved faithfulness and explanation quality on VQA-NLE benchmarks.
explanation_techniques: natural-language explanations (post-hoc); contrastive learning for faithfulness
evaluation_design: benchmark experiments on VQA-NLE datasets; faithfulness metrics
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=partial (faithful NLE relevant to explanation reliability; not LLM-focused).

title: Role of main RNA modifications in cancer: N6-methyladenosine, 5-methylcytosine, and pseudouridine
task: review RNA modifications (m6A, m5C, pseudouridine) in cancer progression and related mechanisms
abstract: Epigenetic regulation includes chemical modifications that influence gene expression without altering sequence. With advances in sequencing, RNA modifications have become prominent in cancer research. This review summarizes major RNA modifications—N6-methyladenosine (m6A), 5-methylcytosine (m5C), and pseudouridine—covering their writers/erasers/readers, roles in tumorigenesis, and potential diagnostic and therapeutic implications.
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: Emojis as Pictures
task: argue emojis function as pictures and propose a pictorial semantics with pragmatic enrichment
abstract: This paper argues emojis are essentially small pictures rather than words or gestures. It develops a pictorial semantics based on geometric projection with abstraction and stylization, and adds an account of pragmatic enrichment driven by coherence and non-literal interpretation. It analyzes differences between emojis depicting entities versus facial expressions as truth-conditional versus use-conditional pictorial content.
evaluation_design: theory paper
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=no.

title: Quantum machine learning for chemistry and physics
task: review QML methods and applications in chemistry and physics; discuss algorithms and near-term prospects
model: quantum ML models (VQCs, quantum kernels, hybrid methods)
abstract: Machine learning has driven advances across the physical sciences, and quantum computing offers new opportunities for learning and simulation. This review surveys quantum machine learning methods relevant to chemistry and physics, including hybrid quantum–classical approaches, variational circuits, quantum kernels, and representative applications, discussing current capabilities and limitations for near-term quantum hardware.
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: On equivariant topological modular forms
task: construct integral equivariant elliptic cohomology and equivariant spectra of topological modular forms; compute fixed points
abstract: Following ideas of Lurie, this paper constructs an integral theory of equivariant elliptic cohomology for arbitrary compact Lie groups. Specializing to the universal elliptic curve yields equivariant spectra of topological modular forms (TMF) for compact abelian Lie groups, and the paper proves structural properties (e.g., symmetric monoidality for circle-equivariant elliptic cohomology) and computes fixed points for the circle group and more general tori.
evaluation_design: theoretical mathematics
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=no.

title: Ascending neurons convey behavioral state to integrative sensory and action selection brain regions
task: map encoding and brain targets of ascending neurons conveying behavioral state signals in Drosophila
abstract: Knowing one’s behavioral state helps contextualize sensory cues and select appropriate actions, but the cellular sources of such state signals are unclear. Using large-scale functional imaging in behaving flies and morphological quantification, this study characterizes hundreds of genetically identifiable ascending neurons, showing they encode behavioral states (e.g., self-motion) and target distinct brain hubs involved in sensory integration and action selection; projection patterns in the motor system predict encoding.
evaluation_design: experimental neuroscience study
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=no.

title: Mirror symmetry for very affine hypersurfaces
task: develop approach to homological mirror symmetry for very affine hypersurfaces
abstract: This paper develops an approach to homological mirror symmetry for very affine hypersurfaces, building on tools such as wrapped Fukaya categories, skeleta and sectors, and sheaf-theoretic methods. It introduces the relevant geometric setup (tori, Landau–Ginzburg models) and proves equivalences in the mirror correspondence for this class of varieties.
evaluation_design: theoretical mathematics
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=no.

title: Neurodegenerative Disease Tauopathies
venue: Annual Review of Pathology: Mechanisms of Disease
task: review tauopathies: pathological tau aggregation, clinical phenotypes, mechanisms, and therapeutic directions
abstract: Tauopathies are progressive neurodegenerative diseases characterized by aberrant tau inclusions in the central nervous system. This review summarizes how tau forms pathological fibrillar aggregates associated with neuronal loss and diverse clinical phenotypes (e.g., dementia and movement disorders), and discusses current mechanistic understanding, disease heterogeneity, biomarkers, and emerging therapeutic approaches.
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: The role of cohomology in quantum computation with magic states
task: connect cohomological structures to magic-state quantum computation, quasiprobability negativity, and contextuality
abstract: Cohomological structures relate quantum error correction, measurement-based computation, symmetry-protected topological order, and contextuality. This paper extends these connections to magic-state quantum computation, analyzing how negativity of certain quasiprobability representations serves as an indicator of quantumness and highlighting differences between even- and odd-dimensional local Hilbert spaces in constructing such representations.
evaluation_design: theoretical
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=no.

title: Modeling Multi-hop Question Answering as Single Sequence Prediction
venue: ACL (Annual Meeting of the Association for Computational Linguistics)
task: multi-hop QA with generative single-sequence prediction extending Fusion-in-Decoder with explicit reasoning paths
abstract: Multi-hop question answering requires combining evidence from multiple documents, which challenges generative QA approaches. This paper proposes a generative method (PATH-FID) that models multi-hop QA as single-sequence prediction while explicitly modeling reasoning paths beyond answer generation, improving effectiveness on multi-hop QA benchmarks compared to standard Fusion-in-Decoder baselines.
evaluation_design: benchmark experiments on multi-hop QA datasets
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=partial (NLP reasoning/path modeling; not explainability-focused).

title: Alzheimer’s disease – the journey of a healthy brain into organ failure
task: review AD pathogenesis from Aβ aggregation to tau pathology, cellular dysfunction, and neurodegeneration; highlight gaps
abstract: Alzheimer’s disease (AD) is a complex degenerative proteinopathy that can be clinically detected in living patients, but major gaps remain in understanding and treatment. This review summarizes key mechanistic insights from Aβ aggregation through neurofibrillary tangle pathology and multi-cell-type brain dysfunction to overt neurodegeneration, emphasizing unmet medical need and research gaps across the lifespan.
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: Towards Process-Oriented, Modular, and Versatile Question Generation that Meets Educational Needs
task: analyze barriers to classroom adoption of QG and propose process-oriented modular framework aligned to instructor workflows
abstract: Automatic question generation (QG) can support education, but adoption in classrooms is low. This work identifies key impediments by studying how instructors construct questions and the touchpoints where QG tools can fit educational workflows, motivating process-oriented, modular and versatile QG approaches aimed at improving usability for educational needs.
evaluation_design: analysis + framework; may include empirical studies with instructors
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=no.

title: Maintenance of pluripotency-like signature in the entire ectoderm leads to neural crest stem cell potential
task: track transcriptional changes gastrulation→neurulation and link ectoderm-wide pluripotency-like signatures to neural crest potential
abstract: Neural crest cells arise from ectoderm yet can generate derivatives beyond typical ectodermal capacity, and the origin of this high potential is unclear. Using single-cell multiplex spatial transcriptomics and complementary assays, this study tracks transcriptional changes from gastrulation to neurulation and reports maintenance of a pluripotency-like signature across ectoderm late into neurulation that later becomes restricted, proposing a mechanism for enhanced stemness and neural crest potential.
evaluation_design: single-cell spatial transcriptomics + developmental experiments
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=no.

title: The spatial distribution of an aromatic molecule, C6H5CN, in the cold dark cloud TMC-1
task: map spatial extent of benzonitrile in TMC-1 and compare with cyanopolyynes and radicals; infer formation pathways
abstract: We present a highly sensitive 2D line survey of TMC-1 obtained with the Yebes 40 m radio telescope in the Q-band. We report the first determination of the spatial extent of benzonitrile (C6H5CN), showing it follows cyanopolyynes but differs from radicals CnH and CnN, supporting formation of aromatic species via reactions involving smaller species in the densest cloud zones.
evaluation_design: observational study (radio mapping)
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=no.

title: Epigenetic inheritance is unfaithful at intermediately methylated CpG sites
task: quantify fidelity of DNA methylation inheritance at nucleotide level in clonal somatic lines
abstract: DNA methylation at CpG sites is often considered stable through clonal expansion. Using high-throughput bisulfite sequencing of clonally derived somatic cell lines, this study measures methylation inheritance at nucleotide resolution and finds high fidelity at hypo- and hypermethylated sites but low fidelity at intermediately methylated CpGs, which are widespread across the genome and within genes.
evaluation_design: experimental genomics
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=no.

title: Organ-on-a-chip meets artificial intelligence in drug evaluation
task: review organ-on-a-chip platforms and how AI improves OoC design and data processing for drug testing
model: AI methods for design/analysis (ML for OoC data)
abstract: Organ-on-a-chip (OoC) technology can reproduce organ-level physiology on microfluidic chips and offers promise for innovating drug development beyond animal models. This review surveys progress in OoC platforms and applications (e.g., drug testing), and discusses how advances in artificial intelligence improve OoC design and data processing, including for single- and multi-organ chips.
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=partial (AI-in-bioengineering; not LLM explainability).

title: Intragenomic rDNA variation - the product of concerted evolution, mutation, or something in between?
task: review evidence and mechanisms for partial/failed rDNA homogenization and intragenomic variation
abstract: Concerted evolution posits homogenization of ribosomal DNA (rDNA) repeats, but high-throughput sequencing reveals partial and sometimes failing homogenization in some organisms. This review summarizes evidence for intragenomic rDNA variation and discusses candidate processes (e.g., mutation, recombination and selection) that can lead to unexpected diversity among repeats.
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: Anomalies and symmetry fractionalization
task: analyze how one-form symmetries and fractionalization classes affect ’t Hooft anomalies of ordinary symmetries
abstract: We study an ordinary symmetry G and its anomalies in systems with a one-form symmetry Γ. In such theories, G’s action on charged line operators requires additional data (a fractionalization class), and different choices can change anomaly values when Γ is involved. We show multiple anomaly values can be realized via twisted gauge transformations that shift fractionalization classes, and illustrate the framework in QCD-like theories across dimensions.
evaluation_design: theoretical
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=no.

title: Transcription–Replication Conflicts as a Source of Genome Instability
task: review transcription–replication conflicts, mechanisms, consequences for genome instability, and mitigation pathways
abstract: Replication and transcription machineries cannot simultaneously act on the same DNA template, creating transcription–replication conflicts (TRCs) that can cause genome instability. This review summarizes sources and mechanisms of TRCs in prokaryotes and eukaryotes, how they lead to DNA damage and mutagenesis, and cellular pathways that prevent or resolve conflicts to maintain genome integrity.
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: Between Wisdom and Apocalypse: Reading Tosefta Soṭah Chapters 10–15
venue: Harvard Theological Review (likely; verify in PDF)
task: argue Tosefta Sotah 10–15 forms a coherent aggadic unit combining apocalyptic, priestly, and wisdom themes
abstract: Chapters 10–15 in Tosefta Soṭah contain an unusually long aggadic unit combining loosely connected materials. This article argues the unit is coherent, weaving apocalyptic, priestly, and wisdom themes to narrate perpetual decline from a biblical golden age to an age of destruction with an eschatological remedy, offering an alternative to standard rabbinic narratives of the shift from prophecy to Torah.
evaluation_design: humanities analysis
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=no.

title: Prompt-dependent performance of multimodal AI model in oral diagnosis: a comprehensive analysis of accuracy, narrative quality, calibration, and latency versus human experts
domain: medical multimodal LLM evaluation / oral diagnosis
task: compare diagnostic performance of a multimodal LLM under different prompting strategies vs human experts on histopathology-verified oral cases
abstract: Prompt design can strongly influence diagnostic performance of multimodal LLMs. This prospective paired study evaluates Gemini Pro 2.5 under multiple prompting strategies on histopathology-verified oral medicine vignettes, comparing accuracy, narrative quality, calibration, and latency against expert clinicians. Results show structured prompting affects performance and interpretability trade-offs, with chain-of-thought style prompts improving performance in complex cases while experts remain superior on straightforward cases.
explanation_techniques: prompting strategies (direct vs structured; chain-of-thought)
evaluation_design: prospective diagnostic accuracy study; blinded expert scoring; calibration metrics
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=yes (prompting effects on reliability/interpretability in clinical multimodal LLMs).

title: Assessing the performance of large language models in literature screening for pharmacovigilance: a comparative study
venue: Frontiers in Drug Safety and Regulation
task: evaluate LLMs (GPT-3.5/4, Claude2) for classifying PV literature as relevant/irrelevant; study prompt/keyword effects
abstract: Manual literature screening for pharmacovigilance is labor-intensive. This study evaluates large language models (GPT-3.5, GPT-4, Claude2) for categorizing publications as relevant vs irrelevant for safety signal reviews under different prompting strategies (N-shot examples, chain-of-thought), reporting high reproducibility and sensitivity with moderate specificity, and analyzing factors such as keywords and prompt design that influence outcomes.
explanation_techniques: chain-of-thought prompting; example-based prompts
evaluation_design: comparative evaluation on PV screening dataset; accuracy/sensitivity/specificity; reproducibility analysis
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=yes (LLM screening + prompting effects).

title: Augmenting large language models with chemistry tools
task: introduce ChemCrow, an LLM chemistry agent integrating expert tools to solve synthesis/drug/material tasks
abstract: LLMs perform well broadly but struggle on chemistry tasks and lack access to external knowledge. This paper introduces ChemCrow, a GPT-4–based chemistry agent that integrates 18 expert-designed tools to support tasks in organic synthesis, drug discovery, and materials design. With tool augmentation, new capabilities emerge and the agent can autonomously plan and execute chemistry workflows, improving correctness and usefulness for scientific applications.
explanation_techniques: tool-augmented agent traces (plans/tool calls)
evaluation_design: task-based evaluation across chemistry problems; case studies of autonomous planning/execution
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=partial (LLM tool-use/agents; explainability indirect via traces).

title: Embracing the future of Artificial Intelligence in the classroom: the relevance of AI literacy, prompt engineering, and critical thinking in modern education
venue: International Journal of Educational Technology in Higher Education
task: discuss AI in education and argue for AI literacy, prompt engineering skills, and critical thinking for learners/educators
abstract: This article discusses the impact of AI in education and argues that effective integration requires AI literacy (understanding AI technologies and societal impacts), prompt engineering skills for interacting with generative systems, and strengthened critical thinking. It highlights opportunities for personalized support as well as challenges around teacher training and curriculum adaptation.
evaluation_design: perspective/review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=partial (prompting in education; not LLM explainability methods).

title: Common Method Bias: It's Bad, It's Complex, It's Widespread, and It's Not Easy to Fix
venue: Annual Review of Organizational Psychology and Organizational Behavior
task: review common method bias (sources, consequences, and remedies) and provide recommendations for control
abstract: Common method bias (CMB) can distort observed relationships in behavioral research, but its causes and remedies are often misunderstood. This review summarizes why CMB is harmful, discusses multiple sources that may co-occur in studies, reviews procedural and statistical remedies and their limitations, and provides recommendations for diagnosing and controlling CMB in research practice.
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: The evolution of SARS-CoV-2
task: review mechanisms generating SARS-CoV-2 genetic variation and selective forces shaping variants (transmissibility, immune evasion)
abstract: This review summarizes how genetic variation in SARS-CoV-2 arises and is shaped within hosts and at the population level. It discusses evolutionary events during the pandemic, including emergence of variants with changes in transmissibility, severity, and immune evasion, and examines mechanisms and selective forces that drove major evolutionary phases.
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: Role of neuroinflammation in neurodegeneration development
task: review how neuroinflammation contributes to neurodegenerative disease initiation/progression and interacts with protein aggregation
abstract: Inflammation is not only a consequence of neurodegeneration but can also drive disease development. This review summarizes evidence that protein aggregates can induce neuroinflammation that in turn aggravates aggregation and neurodegeneration, and discusses how genetic variation in CNS cells and peripheral immune influences can trigger early inflammatory processes, outlining implicated CNS cell types and signaling pathways.
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: Targeting integrin pathways: mechanisms and advances in therapy
task: review integrin signaling mechanisms and therapeutic targeting advances
abstract: Integrins are transmembrane adhesion receptors that link the extracellular matrix to the cytoskeleton and transduce biochemical and mechanical signals. This review summarizes mechanisms regulating integrin activation/inactivation (protein interactions, conformational change, trafficking) and surveys therapeutic strategies targeting integrin pathways, discussing progress and challenges over decades of drug development.
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: Pseudomonas aeruginosa: pathogenesis, virulence factors, antibiotic resistance, interaction with host, technology advances and emerging therapeutics
task: comprehensive review of P. aeruginosa pathogenesis, virulence, resistance, host interactions, and emerging therapeutics
abstract: Pseudomonas aeruginosa is a multidrug-resistant opportunistic pathogen and model bacterium causing severe infections in vulnerable populations. This review surveys virulence factors and regulatory systems, antibiotic resistance mechanisms, host–pathogen interactions and immune networks, and technological advances (omics, scRNA-seq, nanoparticles, screening, phage therapy) that inform development of new therapeutics against resistant strains.
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: Lactate metabolism in human health and disease
task: review lactate roles beyond glycolysis byproduct: shuttling, signaling, lactylation; implications in cancer, inflammation, neurobiology
abstract: This review summarizes the evolving view of lactate from a glycolysis byproduct to a key metabolite and signaling molecule. It discusses the lactate shuttle hypothesis, lactylation as a post-translational modification, and how lactate-related mechanisms contribute to tumor metabolism, neural activity, inflammation and other physiological and pathological processes, highlighting open questions and therapeutic implications.
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: Is Metaverse in education a blessing or a curse: a combined content and bibliometric analysis
task: systematic literature review of metaverse in education with content + bibliometric analysis; identify trends and gaps
abstract: This study conducts a systematic literature review of the metaverse in education and applies combined content and bibliometric analyses to characterize research trends, focal themes, and limitations. It identifies research gaps (e.g., lifelogging applications and other underexplored areas) and discusses opportunities and challenges for educational metaverse deployments.
evaluation_design: systematic review + bibliometric analysis
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: The Economic Impacts of the US–China Trade War
task: review empirical evidence on impacts of US–China trade war tariffs on prices, welfare, firms, and trade patterns
abstract: This review summarizes evidence on the 2018–2019 US–China trade war, in which the US imposed tariffs on hundreds of billions of dollars of imports and China retaliated. Using diverse data and methods, economists assess how tariffs affected US prices and consumers, firm behavior and supply chains, trade flows, and broader welfare and distributional impacts.
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: Transfer learning: a friendly introduction
task: introductory survey of transfer learning concepts, taxonomies, applications, and challenges
abstract: Transfer learning (TL) enables leveraging knowledge from related tasks or domains to improve performance when target data are limited or distributions differ. This paper provides an accessible introduction to TL, summarizing definitions, categories and workflows, representative algorithms and applications, and common challenges such as negative transfer and domain shift.
evaluation_design: tutorial/survey
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=partial (ML background; not LLM explainability).

title: The effects of the COVID-19 pandemic on community respiratory virus activity
task: review how COVID-19 non-pharmaceutical interventions and vaccination altered circulation of other respiratory viruses
abstract: Non-pharmaceutical interventions implemented during the COVID-19 pandemic altered transmission of many respiratory viruses. This review summarizes observed reductions and shifts in community infections (e.g., influenza and RSV), how patterns varied by virus type and over subsequent pandemic waves, and implications for hospitalizations, mortality, population immunity and future respiratory virus dynamics.
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: Polyvagal Theory: A Science of Safety
task: review polyvagal theory framing feelings of safety as autonomic-state dependent; propose measurable neurophysiological substrate
abstract: This review argues that feelings of safety emerge from internal physiological states regulated by the autonomic nervous system, and that health strategies often neglect these biological needs. Polyvagal Theory is presented as a framework to study safety as an objective, measurable construct grounded in neurophysiology, offering implications for research on stress, trauma, and wellbeing.
evaluation_design: review/theory
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: Research progress of glutathione peroxidase family (GPX) in redoxidation
task: review GPX enzyme family roles in redox balance, cell fate, and disease; summarize research progress
abstract: Maintaining cellular redox balance is key to cell fate, and glutathione peroxidases (GPXs) are central components of antioxidant defense. This review summarizes GPX family members, their biochemical functions in redox regulation, roles across tissues and diseases, and pharmacological perspectives, highlighting recent progress and remaining challenges.
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: Lactobacillus spp. for Gastrointestinal Health: Current and Future Perspectives
task: review evidence for Lactobacillus probiotics in GI health, mechanisms, and future directions including personalization
abstract: Probiotic bacteria are widely used to modulate human health, with Lactobacillus species among the most studied for gastrointestinal benefits. This review summarizes evidence for Lactobacillus-associated GI effects, discusses proposed mechanisms and host–microbe interactions, and highlights future research needs, including understanding inter-individual variability and improving mechanistic and clinical evidence.
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: Human-like intuitive behavior and reasoning biases emerged in large language models but disappeared in ChatGPT
task: test GPT-family models on semantic illusions and cognitive reflection tasks; compare bias patterns across model generations
abstract: The authors design semantic illusion items and cognitive reflection tests that elicit intuitive but erroneous responses in humans, and administer them to OpenAI GPT models. As models increase in size and linguistic proficiency, they show more human-like intuitive “System 1” errors; this pattern shifts with ChatGPT models, which more often avoid the embedded traps and respond correctly.
evaluation_design: behavioral evaluation benchmark; error/bias analysis across models
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=yes (LLM reasoning biases; robustness/overreliance implications).

title: Social exchange theory: Systematic review and future directions
task: systematic review of social exchange theory literature and propose future research directions
abstract: Social exchange theory (SET) is widely used to explain social and organizational behavior. This systematic review synthesizes recent SET research, summarizes core constructs and application areas, and identifies gaps and future directions for theory development and empirical study.
evaluation_design: systematic review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: Cross-Task Generalization via Natural Language Crowdsourcing Instructions
venue: ACL (Annual Meeting of the Association for Computational Linguistics)
task: study cross-task generalization using natural-language task instructions sourced from crowdsourcing; improve task transfer
abstract: Humans can solve diverse tasks by reading natural-language instructions and a few examples, but supervised models often fail to generalize across tasks. This work studies cross-task generalization by leveraging natural-language instructions collected from crowdsourcing for many tasks, training instruction-conditioned models to better transfer to new tasks and settings.
evaluation_design: benchmark experiments across multiple NLP tasks
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=partial (instruction-following relevant; not explainability-focused).

title: Notch signaling pathway: architecture, disease, and therapeutics
task: review Notch signaling mechanisms, context-dependent roles in disease (including cancer), and therapeutic targeting strategies
abstract: Notch signaling is an evolutionarily conserved pathway in which receptors undergo regulated cleavages and nuclear translocation to control transcription. This review summarizes Notch pathway architecture, its roles in development and tissue homeostasis, and how dysregulation contributes to cancer and noncancer diseases, emphasizing strong context dependence and surveying therapeutic strategies and challenges.
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: Generalization in quantum machine learning from few training data
task: analyze generalization error scaling in variational quantum machine learning trained with limited data
abstract: Variational quantum machine learning optimizes parameterized quantum circuits on limited training data and must generalize to unseen data. This paper studies generalization performance when training on N samples, providing bounds showing generalization error scales at worst as T/N for circuits with T trainable gates, and tighter behavior when only a small subset of gates substantially changes during optimization.
evaluation_design: theoretical analysis + experiments/simulations
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=no.

title: Conviction Narrative Theory: A theory of choice under radical uncertainty
task: propose conviction narrative theory for decision-making under radical uncertainty emphasizing imagination and social context
abstract: This paper proposes Conviction Narrative Theory to explain how people make choices under radical uncertainty. It emphasizes the role of imagination and social context in generating conviction narratives, arguing that the dynamics between these elements shape individuals’ confidence and decisions when probabilities and outcomes are ill-specified.
evaluation_design: theory/target article
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=no.

title: Targeting cell death pathways for cancer therapy: recent developments in necroptosis, pyroptosis, ferroptosis, and cuproptosis research
task: review regulated cell death modalities beyond apoptosis and implications for cancer therapy and tumor microenvironment immunogenicity
abstract: Because many tumors evade apoptosis, alternative regulated cell death (RCD) pathways have become important therapeutic targets. This review summarizes recent advances in necroptosis, pyroptosis, ferroptosis, and cuproptosis in cancer, including mechanisms, therapeutic strategies, and evidence that RCD can alter tumor microenvironment immunogenicity and treatment responses.
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: Toward low-cost biological and hybrid biological/catalytic conversion of cellulosic biomass to fuels
task: review pathways and techno-economic challenges for cellulosic biomass to fuels using biological and hybrid catalytic approaches
abstract: This review discusses strategies to achieve economically viable, scalable conversion of cellulosic biomass to fuels. It surveys biological routes (e.g., consolidated bioprocessing), hybrid biological/catalytic schemes, key process bottlenecks and cost drivers, and research directions to reduce conversion costs while improving yields and sustainability.
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: The influence of supply chain partners’ integrations on organizational performance: The moderating role of trust
task: test effect of supply chain partner integration on organizational performance and moderation by trust
abstract: Supply chain integration with key partners is increasingly important for organizational performance. This quantitative study examines how integration with supply chain partners affects organizational performance and tests trust as a moderating factor, using survey data from the food sector and statistical analysis to estimate main and interaction effects.
evaluation_design: quantitative survey study (moderation analysis)
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=no.

title: Advancing human gut microbiota research by considering gut transit time
task: review evidence that gut transit time shapes microbiota composition/activity and propose methods/implications for microbiome studies
abstract: Accumulating evidence indicates gut transit time is a key factor shaping gut microbiota composition and activity, with implications for human health. This review summarizes population and within-individual findings linking transit time to microbial ecology and metabolites, discusses measurement approaches and confounding, and argues that accounting for transit time can improve interpretation, reproducibility, and causal inference in microbiome research.
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: Pangenome graph construction from genome alignments with Minigraph-Cactus
task: construct pangenome graphs from genome alignments; present Minigraph-Cactus method and evaluate on human pangenome
abstract: Pangenome references reduce biases of single linear reference genomes by representing diverse haplotypes. This paper introduces Minigraph-Cactus, a method to construct pangenome graphs from multiple genome alignments, enabling scalable graph building for large cohorts (e.g., the Human Pangenome Reference Consortium) and supporting downstream variation analysis and mapping.
evaluation_design: method + empirical evaluation on pangenome datasets
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=no.

title: Social capital I: measurement and associations with economic mobility
task: measure social capital using large-scale social network data and relate components to economic mobility and outcomes
abstract: Social capital—strength of social networks and communities—may influence outcomes like education and health, but measurement has been limited by lack of network data. Using large-scale social network data, this paper proposes measures of social capital and studies associations with economic mobility and related outcomes, distinguishing facets such as economic connectedness and network structure.
evaluation_design: large-scale observational analysis
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=no.

title: Health system-scale language models are all-purpose prediction engines
task: train and evaluate a health system-scale language model for diverse clinical and operational prediction tasks
abstract: Clinical predictive models support time-constrained decisions but are often built for single tasks. This paper presents a health system-scale language model trained on large EHR corpora and shows it can serve as an all-purpose prediction engine, producing representations that support many clinical and operational forecasting tasks with minimal task-specific modeling.
evaluation_design: retrospective evaluation across multiple prediction tasks; benchmarking vs task-specific baselines
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=yes (clinical LMs; generalization + evaluation).

title: Elesclomol: a copper ionophore targeting mitochondrial metabolism for cancer therapy
venue: Journal of Experimental & Clinical Cancer Research
task: review/overview elesclomol mechanism as copper ionophore inducing cuproptosis; discuss mitochondrial metabolism dependence and trial context
abstract: Elesclomol targets mitochondrial metabolism and transports extracellular copper ions, inducing oxidative stress and, more recently recognized, cuproptosis. This review discusses why cancers with high mitochondrial dependence (e.g., stem-like or drug-resistant states) may be especially sensitive, summarizes mechanistic insights and biomarker considerations, and revisits clinical trial outcomes in light of the cuproptosis mechanism.
evaluation_design: review/overview
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: The effect of exercise on cytokines: implications for musculoskeletal health: a narrative review
venue: BMC Sports Science, Medicine and Rehabilitation
task: review cytokine responses to exercise and implications for inflammation modulation and musculoskeletal health
abstract: Exercise has broad cardiovascular and musculoskeletal benefits but can also induce physiological stress and immune activation. This narrative review summarizes evidence linking exercise to cytokine signaling, describing diverse cytokine responses that can propagate, modulate, or mitigate inflammation, and discusses implications for musculoskeletal health and mechanisms underlying exercise-related immune effects.
evaluation_design: narrative review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: War of the chatbots: Bard, Bing Chat, ChatGPT, Ernie and beyond. The new AI gold rush and its impact on higher education
task: discuss rapid chatbot/LLM developments and implications for higher education, including hype cycles, policy and pedagogy impacts
abstract: This article surveys the rapid emergence of chatbot systems since late 2022 (e.g., ChatGPT, Bing Chat, Bard, Ernie) and frames a “gold rush” and arms race affecting higher education. It discusses adoption by students and academics, hype and overclaims, emerging policies and ethical concerns, and potential impacts on teaching, assessment, and institutional strategy.
evaluation_design: commentary/overview
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=partial (education impacts; not explainability methods).

title: Understanding specific ion effects and the Hofmeister series
task: review specific ion effects and Hofmeister series across interfaces, solvation, and macromolecular systems; unify mechanisms
abstract: Specific ion effects (SIEs), including the Hofmeister series, are ubiquitous in chemistry and biology. This perspective reviews historical context and modern understanding of SIE mechanisms across aqueous interfaces, solvation structure, and macromolecular interactions, synthesizing experimental and computational insights and outlining open challenges for predictive theory.
evaluation_design: perspective/review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: Condensates formed by prion-like low-complexity domains have small-world network structures and interfaces defined by expanded conformations
task: characterize structural organization of condensates formed by prion-like low-complexity domains (PLCDs) under sticker-spacer architecture
abstract: Biomolecular condensates can arise via coupled phase separation and percolation of multivalent macromolecules. This study characterizes molecular and mesoscale structure of condensates formed by intrinsically disordered prion-like low-complexity domains (PLCDs) with sticker–spacer architectures, describing network organization (small-world properties) and how expanded conformations define condensate interfaces.
evaluation_design: biophysical modeling + experiments/simulations
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=no.

title: Antibodies to combat viral infections: development strategies and progress
task: review monoclonal antibody development for viral therapeutics and prophylaxis; engineering, structural selection, lessons from COVID-19
abstract: Monoclonal antibodies (mAbs) are attractive therapeutics and prophylactics for viral infections due to high specificity and immune effector functions. This review summarizes strategies for identifying and optimizing neutralizing mAbs (including structural biology–guided targeting of vulnerable epitopes), antibody engineering to improve effector function and half-life, and progress accelerated by COVID-19 mAb development, with implications for other infectious diseases and vaccine design.
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: A Separate Reality: An Update on Place Illusion and Plausibility in Virtual Reality
task: review presence as Place Illusion and Plausibility; update evidence and implications for VR design and experiments
abstract: This review updates the concept of presence in virtual reality as two orthogonal illusions: Place Illusion (PI, feeling located in the depicted place) and Plausibility (Psi, feeling that events are really happening), even while users know the situation is illusory. It surveys factors that elicit PI and Psi and discusses implications for VR research, interaction design, and applications.
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: How understanding large language models can inform the use of ChatGPT in physics education
task: introduce physics education community to LLM functioning; demonstrate prompt-engineering effects on conceptual physics tasks; discuss implications
abstract: This paper introduces large language models (LLMs) for the physics education community, summarizes prior work on ChatGPT performance on physics tasks, and provides an accessible account of how LLMs work. It presents examples showing how prompt engineering changes LLM performance on conceptual physics questions, and discusses strengths, limitations, and implications for teaching and learning.
evaluation_design: didactic review + illustrative experiments
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=partial (prompting and evaluation in education; not explainability methods).

title: Foundation metrics for evaluating effectiveness of healthcare conversations powered by generative AI
task: propose metrics for evaluating generative-AI healthcare chatbots effectiveness and safety across clinical conversation dimensions
abstract: Generative AI chatbots may transform healthcare by enabling personalized, efficient patient-centered conversations, but evaluation remains fragmented. This perspective proposes “foundation metrics” to assess effectiveness and safety of healthcare conversations powered by generative AI, covering dimensions such as clinical correctness, communication quality, user experience, and risk/harms, aiming to support standardized benchmarking and monitoring.
evaluation_design: perspective
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=yes (evaluation metrics for clinical genAI conversations).

title: Shared computational principles for language processing in humans and deep language models
task: compare neural and behavioral signatures of human language processing with deep language models; identify shared computational principles
abstract: Deep language models trained on next-word prediction achieve strong language performance, raising questions about overlap with human language processing. This work compares representations and predictive signals from deep language models with human brain and behavioral data during natural language comprehension, reporting shared computational principles and identifying where model predictions align with neural responses.
evaluation_design: neural/behavioral alignment study
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=yes (human–LM alignment; interpretability of model representations).

title: Amino acid metabolism in immune cells: essential regulators of the effector functions, and promising opportunities to enhance cancer immunotherapy
task: review how amino acid metabolism regulates immune cell differentiation/function in tumor microenvironment and therapeutic opportunities
abstract: Amino acids are key nutrients for immune cells, and dysregulated amino-acid consumption in the tumor microenvironment can impair anti-tumor immunity. This review summarizes how altered amino acid metabolism, transporters, enzymes, and sensors (e.g., mTOR, GCN2) govern immune cell fate, contribute to tumor outgrowth and therapy resistance, and outlines strategies to enhance immunotherapy via supplementation or targeting metabolic pathways.
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs): advances in structures, mechanisms and drug discovery
task: review GPCR structural advances, activation mechanisms, functional diversity; summarize recent FDA-approved orthosteric drugs and interactions
abstract: GPCRs are the largest family of human membrane proteins and major drug targets with complex signaling features (orthosteric/allosteric, biased/balanced). This review summarizes structural advances, activation mechanisms, and functional diversity of GPCRs, and focuses on GPCR drug discovery by detailing drug–target interactions and mechanisms of recent FDA-approved orthosteric GPCR drugs.
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: Geographic and seasonal variation of blue whale calls in the North Pacific
venue: Journal of Cetacean Research and Management (or JCRM)
task: analyze hydrophone recordings across North Pacific to characterize geographic and seasonal patterns of blue whale call types
abstract: Using acoustic surveys from hydrophone arrays across sixteen North Pacific sites, this study examines blue whale call characteristics and distribution. It identifies two distinct call types with clear geographic and seasonal variation: western sites predominantly record a “northwestern” call type, eastern sites a “northeastern” type, and central sites both, with differing seasonal windows across regions.
evaluation_design: observational acoustic analysis
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=no.

title: Causality of genetically determined metabolites on anxiety disorders: a two-sample Mendelian randomization study
task: use two-sample Mendelian randomization to assess causal effects of genetically determined metabolites on anxiety disorders
abstract: This study explores causal relationships between genetically determined metabolites and anxiety disorders using two-sample Mendelian randomization. GWAS summary statistics for hundreds of metabolites are used as exposures and multiple anxiety-disorder GWAS datasets as outcomes, with instrumental-variable analysis aiming to identify metabolites with potential causal roles and shed light on biological mechanisms.
evaluation_design: Mendelian randomization (two-sample) over GWAS datasets
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=no.

title: Calcium carbonate: controlled synthesis, surface functionalization, and nanostructured materials
task: review controlled synthesis and surface modification of CaCO3, stabilization of amorphous CaCO3, and CaCO3-based nanostructured materials
abstract: Calcium carbonate is widespread in biological and geological systems and widely used industrially. This review surveys recent advances in controlled CaCO3 synthesis and surface functionalization, stabilization of amorphous CaCO3, and development of CaCO3-based nanostructured materials, highlighting mechanisms, characterization, and application directions.
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: Autophagy: Regulator of cell death
task: review how autophagy regulates cell death; distinguish autophagy-dependent vs autophagy-mediated death; summarize modalities and mechanisms
abstract: Autophagy degrades and recycles cellular components to maintain homeostasis and is often protective, but dysregulation or excessive flux can lead to cell death. This review discusses how autophagy regulates cell death, distinguishes autophagy-dependent and autophagy-mediated cell death, and surveys major autophagy-related cell-death modalities and their molecular mechanisms, highlighting open questions.
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: The impending impacts of large language models on medical education
task: commentary on how LLMs impact medical education; describe capabilities (translation, summarization, grammar correction) and implications/risks
abstract: This commentary summarizes large language models (LLMs) as systems trained on massive text corpora that can perform translation, summarization, and writing assistance. It discusses how scaling affects capabilities, outlines opportunities and risks for medical education (assessment, learning support, misinformation), and calls for educators to adapt curricula and evaluation practices in response to LLM adoption.
evaluation_design: commentary
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=partial (education impacts).

title: A peripheral signature of Alzheimer’s disease featuring microbiota-gut-brain axis markers
task: test associations between gut microbiota and microbiota–gut–brain axis mediators with amyloid cascade in sporadic AD
abstract: This study examines whether gut microbiota and microbiota–gut–brain axis mediators are associated with the amyloid cascade in sporadic Alzheimer’s disease. Across cognitively impaired AD, cognitively impaired non-AD, and unimpaired groups, it analyzes fecal microbiota via 16S rRNA sequencing and blood markers (e.g., LPS, endothelial/immune markers) to identify peripheral signatures linked to AD pathology.
evaluation_design: case-control observational study
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=no.

title: Immune checkpoint modulators in cancer immunotherapy: recent advances and emerging concepts
task: review immune checkpoint molecules beyond PD-1/PD-L1 and CTLA-4 (e.g., VISTA, CD39/CD73/CD38) and therapeutic advances
abstract: Immune checkpoint inhibitors targeting PD-1/PD-L1 and CTLA-4 have transformed cancer therapy but have limitations in efficacy and toxicity. This review surveys emerging immune checkpoint modulators and concepts, including inhibitory receptors such as VISTA and ectonucleotidases (CD39, CD73, CD38), summarizing preclinical/clinical progress and challenges for next-generation immunotherapies.
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: Global polycrisis: the causal mechanisms of crisis entanglement
task: define polycrisis and develop framework for causal mechanisms entangling global crises
abstract: Multiple global crises (e.g., pandemic, climate change, war) have become entangled in ways that are devastating yet poorly understood. This paper provides a substantive definition of “polycrisis”, differentiates it from related concepts, and proposes a theoretical framework explaining causal mechanisms of crisis entanglement.
evaluation_design: conceptual/theoretical framework
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=no.

title: Towards Unifying Evaluation of Counterfactual Explanations: Leveraging Large Language Models for Human-Centric Assessments
task: propose unified evaluation for counterfactual explanations using LLMs to approximate human-centric assessment and explanatory virtues
abstract: Counterfactual explanations are widely used to support decision-making, but evaluation is fragmented and often lacks user grounding. This paper proposes a unified evaluation approach that leverages large language models to capture human-centric explanatory virtues and assess counterfactual explanations more consistently, aiming to reduce inconsistent findings and support standardized benchmarking.
explanation_techniques: counterfactual explanations; LLM-based evaluators
evaluation_design: evaluation framework + experiments comparing metrics and LLM-assessed criteria
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=yes (LLM-assisted evaluation of XAI).

title: Artificial intelligence in intelligent tutoring systems toward sustainable education: a systematic review
task: systematic review of AI in intelligent tutoring systems with focus on sustainable education goals, challenges, and opportunities
abstract: Sustainable education faces challenges such as limited resources and engagement. This systematic review surveys how artificial intelligence is used in intelligent tutoring systems to support sustainable education, including personalized learning, analytics-driven insights for teachers, and decision support. It summarizes trends, application areas, and open challenges for effective deployment.
evaluation_design: systematic review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=no.

title: Explainable machine learning in materials science
task: introduce XAI concepts for materials scientists; review example applications, challenges and opportunities
model: XAI methods for DNNs and other ML in materials
abstract: Machine learning models are increasingly used in materials research, but many high-accuracy models are hard to interpret. This review provides an entry point to explainable AI for materials scientists: it clarifies what “explain” means in materials contexts, surveys representative XAI methods and example studies, and discusses challenges and opportunities for using explanations to accelerate materials discovery and understanding.
explanation_techniques: feature attribution, surrogate models, example-based explanations (surveyed)
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=partial (XAI background; not LLM explainability).

title: The Shapley Value in Machine Learning
task: survey cooperative game theory foundations and Shapley value applications in ML (explainability, feature selection, data valuation, etc.)
abstract: The Shapley value from cooperative game theory has many ML applications. This survey reviews core game-theoretic concepts and axioms of the Shapley value, then overviews applications including feature selection, explainability, multi-agent reinforcement learning, ensemble pruning, and data valuation. It also discusses key limitations and open research directions.
explanation_techniques: Shapley-based explanations (SHAP and related)
evaluation_design: survey
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=partial (general explainability; relevant background).

title: Learning analytics dashboard: a tool for providing actionable insights to learners
venue: International Journal of Educational Technology in Higher Education
task: review learning analytics dashboards and propose dashboard integrating ML for predictive and prescriptive analytics
abstract: This paper reviews learning analytics dashboards and challenges in operationalization. It analyzes recent dashboards for actionable learner insights and finds many use only descriptive analytics. It then proposes a dashboard that integrates machine learning to support not only descriptive but also predictive and prescriptive analytics, illustrating how modern analytics tools can enable better learner self-regulation.
evaluation_design: review + dashboard proposal
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=no.

title: Navigating the Ethical Challenges of Artificial Intelligence in Higher Education: An Analysis of Seven Global AI Ethics Policies
task: analyze global AI ethics policies for higher education, focusing on transparency, accountability, bias, and governance
abstract: AI use in higher education raises ethical risks including biased algorithms in admissions/grading, transparency and accountability gaps, and workforce displacement. This paper analyzes seven global AI ethics policies relevant to higher education, extracting objectives and principles (e.g., traceability, documentation, responsible development and deployment) and discussing implications for universities and governance.
evaluation_design: policy analysis
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=no.

title: Model agnostic generation of counterfactual explanations for molecules
task: generate counterfactual molecular explanations for black-box chemistry models in a model-agnostic manner
abstract: Deep learning in chemistry often lacks interpretability, and counterfactuals can explain predictions by showing minimal input changes that alter outcomes. This paper proposes a model-agnostic method to generate counterfactual explanations for molecular property prediction, producing chemically valid alternative molecules that flip a model’s prediction and help reveal decision boundaries and spurious correlations.
explanation_techniques: counterfactual explanations for molecules
evaluation_design: method + experiments on molecular prediction tasks
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=partial (counterfactual XAI; not LLM-focused).

title: Social capital II: determinants of economic connectedness
task: analyze determinants of cross-class interaction (economic connectedness) using large-scale Facebook data
abstract: Low levels of interaction across class lines are associated with worse outcomes such as lower upward mobility. Using large-scale Facebook data, this paper analyzes determinants of cross-class interaction (economic connectedness), building on a companion measurement paper. It decomposes drivers of connectedness across communities and settings and identifies factors that may increase cross-class ties.
evaluation_design: large-scale observational analysis
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=no.

title: Towards artificial general intelligence via a multimodal foundation model
task: pretrain multimodal foundation model with weakly correlated internet data for broad downstream cognitive tasks
model: multimodal foundation model (self-supervised; weak semantic correlations)
abstract: To move beyond single-ability AI systems, this work proposes a multimodal foundation model trained on large-scale multimodal data and adapted to diverse downstream cognitive tasks. The model is pretrained via self-supervised learning on weakly semantically correlated internet data, demonstrating promising generalization across tasks as a step toward AGI.
evaluation_design: multitask benchmarking across downstream cognitive tasks
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=partial (foundation model context; not explainability).

title: Human-Centered Design to Address Biases in Artificial Intelligence
task: identify biases across AI lifecycle in healthcare and recommend human-centered design practices to mitigate disparities
abstract: AI can reduce or exacerbate healthcare disparities depending on how it is designed and deployed. This perspective maps potential biases across the AI lifecycle (data collection and annotation through model development, evaluation, deployment, monitoring, and feedback) and recommends involving diverse stakeholders and human-centered AI principles to mitigate bias and improve equity.
evaluation_design: perspective
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=partial (bias/fairness governance).

title: Chemistry-intuitive explanation of graph neural networks for molecular property prediction with substructure masking
task: propose substructure mask explanation (SME) to explain molecular GNN predictions using chemically meaningful substructures
model: graph neural networks; SME explanation method
abstract: GNNs are widely used for molecular property prediction but are hard to explain. Existing chemistry-GNN explanations often attribute to nodes/edges/fragments that are not chemically meaningful. This paper introduces substructure mask explanation (SME), which leverages established molecular segmentation to mask substructures and quantify their contributions, producing chemistry-intuitive explanations for GNN predictions.
explanation_techniques: substructure masking; attribution via chemically meaningful segmentation
evaluation_design: method + experiments on molecular property benchmarks; qualitative chemistry case studies
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=partial (XAI for molecular models).

title: Interpretability and fairness evaluation of deep learning models on MIMIC-IV dataset
task: analyze interpretability, representation bias, and prediction fairness of deep learning models for in-hospital mortality on MIMIC-IV
abstract: Deep learning models on large healthcare datasets raise concerns about interpretability and fairness. Using MIMIC-IV, this study evaluates deep models for in-hospital mortality prediction, analyzing interpretability as well as dataset representation bias and prediction fairness across demographic groups, aiming to surface biases and guide more trustworthy clinical ML deployment.
explanation_techniques: interpretability analyses (feature importance/attribution; model inspection)
evaluation_design: retrospective benchmarking on MIMIC-IV with fairness metrics
notes: secondary_literature=no; in_scope=partial (XAI+fairness in clinical ML; not LLM-focused).

title: Toward Explainable Artificial Intelligence for Precision Pathology
venue: Annual Review of Pathology: Mechanisms of Disease
task: review AI in precision pathology and needs for quantitative, integrative, standardized analysis; discuss explainable AI directions
model: deep learning for histopathology + molecular profiling
abstract: Precision medicine challenges diagnostic pathology to integrate histological images with large molecular profiling data quantitatively and in standardized ways. This review surveys recent AI/deep learning advances for clinical, histological, and molecular data analysis in pathology and discusses directions for explainable AI to improve trust, accountability, and clinical translation in precision pathology workflows.
explanation_techniques: XAI in pathology (attributions, attention, prototypes, uncertainty; discussed)
evaluation_design: review
notes: secondary_literature=yes; in_scope=partial (XAI in medical imaging; background).

title: Explainability for Large Language Models: A Survey
venue: [llm_xai] ACM Transactions on Intelligent Systems and Technology
task: Survey and taxonomy of explainability methods for transformer-based language models
abstract: Latest updates: hps://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3639372 . . Explainability for Large Language Models: A Survey HAIYAN ZHAO,New Jersey Institute of Technology, USA HANJIE CHEN,Johns Hopkins University, USA FAN YANG,Wake Forest University, USA NINGHAO LIU,University of Georgia, USA HUIQIDENG ,Shanghai Jiao Tong University, China HENGYI CAI,Institute of Computing Technology, CAS, China SHUAIQIANG WANGand DAWEI YIN,Baidu Inc., China MENGNAN DU,New Jersey Institute of Technology, USA Large language models (LLMs) have demo
explanation_techniques: taxonomy of local and global explanation methods; fine-tuning-based explanations; prompting-based explanations; evaluation metrics for explanations
evaluation_design: secondary literature review and taxonomy synthesis
main_findings: The survey organizes LLM explainability methods by training paradigm, distinguishing fine-tuning-based and prompting-based approaches, and further separating local explanations for individual outputs from global explanations of model knowledge and behavior. It also highlights evaluation metrics, uses of explanations for debugging and performance improvement, and open challenges specific to LLMs versus earlier deep models.
conclusions: Explainability for LLMs remains fragmented across methods, goals, and evaluation practices, and the field needs stronger benchmarks and metrics tailored to modern prompting-based and instruction-following models. The paper is useful as a secondary source for taxonomy, evaluation dimensions, and open research gaps, rather than as evidence for a single method.
limitations: Secondary survey rather than a primary evaluation study; conclusions depend on the coverage and framing of the surveyed literature. It is strongest for taxonomy and scoping, but not for direct empirical claims about faithfulness or user impact.
notes: Extraction completed semi-automatically from local PDF; verify details during final curation.

title: DriveGPT4: Interpretable End-to-End Autonomous Driving Via Large Language Model
task: Explainable autonomous driving with LLM-based reasoning/interface
abstract: —Multimodallarge language models (MLLMs) have emerged as a prominent area of interest within the research com- munity, given their proficiency in handling and reasoning with non-textual data, including images and videos. This study seeks to extend the application of MLLMs to the realm of autonomous driv- ing by introducing DriveGPT4, a novel interpretable end-to-end autonomous driving system based on LLMs. Capable of processing multi-frame video inputs and textual queries, DriveGPT4 facili- tates the interpretation of vehicle actions, offers pertinent reason- ing, and effectively addresses a diverse range of questions posed by users. Furthermore, DriveGPT4 predicts low-level vehicle control signals in an end-to-end fashion. These advanced capabilities are achieved through the utilization of a bespoke visual instruction tuning dataset, specifically tailored for autonomous driving ap- plications, in conjunction with a mix-finetuning training strategy. DriveGPT4 represents the pioneering effort to leverage LLMs for the development of an interpretable end-to-end autonomous driving solution. Evaluations conducted on the BDD-X dataset showcase the superior qualitative and quantitative performance of DriveGPT4. Additionally, the fine-tuning of domain-specific data enables DriveGPT4 to yield close or even improved results in terms of autonomous driving grounding when contrasted with GPT4-V . title: MentaLLaMA: Interpretable Mental Health Analysis on Social Media with Large Language Models
domain: mental health; social media; interpretable LLM analysis
task: Interpretable mental health analysis on social media
abstract: As an integral part of people’s daily lives, social media is becom- ing a rich source for automatic mental health analysis. As tradi- tional discriminative methods bear poor generalization ability and low interpretability, the recent large language models (LLMs) have been explored for interpretable mental health analysis on social media, which aims to provide detailed explanations along with predictions in zero-shot or few-shot settings. The results show that LLMs still achieve unsatisfactory classification performance in a zero-shot/few-shot manner, which further significantly affects the quality of the generated explanations. Domain-specific finetuning is an effective solution, but faces two critical challenges: 1) lack of high-quality training data. 2) no open-source foundation LLMs. To alleviate these problems, we formally model interpretable men- tal health analysis as a text generation task, and build the first multi-task and multi-source interpretable mental health instruction (IMHI) dataset with 105K data samples to support LLM instruction tuning and evaluation. The raw social media data are collected from 10 existing sources covering 8 mental health analysis tasks. We prompt ChatGPT with expert-designed few-shot prompts to obtain explanations. To ensure the reliability of the explanations, we perform strict automatic and human evaluations on the correct- ness, consistency, and quality of generated data. Based on the IMHI dataset and LLaMA2 foundation models, we train MentaLLaMA, the first open-source instruction-following LLM series for inter- pretable mental health analysis on social media. We evaluate Men- taLLaMA and other advanced methods on the IMHI benchmark, the first holistic evaluation benchmark for interpretable mental health analysis. The results show that MentaLLaMA approaches state-of-the-art discriminative methods in correctness and gener- ates human-level explanations. MentaLLaMA models also show strong generalizability to unseen tasks. The project is available at https://github.com/SteveKGYang/MentaLLaMA. ∗Corresponding author.
